A local retailer sells car batteries and accessories, has provided repair accessories service for 20 years, uses traditional business mode, has loyalty customers limited to local people, owner must hit selling target to get supplier discount leading to over-purchasing, owner and senior worker sometimes too busy to serve customers, new workers cannot recommend battery based on car model, customers call for appointments but owner may forget and schedule senior worker for outstation work. The project aims to create a platform with frontend and backend: frontend provides convenient shopping, product recommendation by car model, shopping cart, payment gateway, self-diagnostic tool, appointment booking; backend supports inventory, order, and work schedule management.

O2O commerce is a business model that moves potential customers from online platforms to physical stores, aiming for seamless digital experience before, during and after purchase. Retailers use advertising and email campaigns to attract online customers to brick-and-mortar stores. O2O combines online and offline benefits for enhanced customer reach, larger market share, and extended marketing capabilities.
business model combining online and offline

Brick-and-mortar refers to a traditional street-side business offering products and services face-to-face in a physical store. They struggle to compete with web-based businesses like Amazon due to lower operating costs and greater flexibility of online retailers. Advantages include in-person shopping experience, important for products needing inspection. Some businesses (hair salons, veterinarians, gas stations, auto repair shops, restaurants, accounting firms) are more suited to brick-and-mortar. Marketing should highlight consumer advantages of physical stores.
